what_it_does: Documents the canonical port-to-service mapping for local AI services, listing each port's runtime, model, endpoint patterns, network binding, and reserved future slots.
observations: Ports 8000/8001/8002 run OpenAI-compatible vLLM chat, embedding, and jina-v4 multimodal embedding services.
Ports 8003-8005 are reserved for future chat/text-only/swap-only siblings tied to specific candidate models.
Port 8080 runs Open WebUI and is described as a web UI, not an API.
Port 11434 runs Ollama and exposes both native /api/* and OpenAI-compatible /v1/* endpoints.
All services bind to 0.0.0.0 so they are reachable from WSL, Windows localhost via WSL2 forwarding, and Docker host networking.
No authentication is configured and traffic is HTTP-only.
Open topics include a future reverse proxy/unified port, HTTPS, and IPv6 binding.
standouts: Explicit 0.0.0.0 binding with a clear security caveat about localhost-only use.
Reserved future ports are already tied to specific candidate models.
Ollama exposes both native and OpenAI-compatible APIs on the same port.
concerns: Binding to 0.0.0.0 with no auth or TLS is unsafe if the host is exposed beyond the local machine.
Reserved ports may be aspirational and could conflict if not actually free.
Open WebUI is said to connect to 8000/8001, but failover or reconfiguration is not described.
notes: This file is the operational lookup table for which local port hosts which model service and how clients should reach it.
questions: How are the services actually started and kept running?
What does 'jina-v4-env' as a runtime specifically mean?
Are the reserved ports already blocked or just planned?
What is 'swap-only chat' intended to mean?
How does Ollama route among its registered models and cloud routes?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a canonical service/port map and also records endpoint, binding, and security context.
intention_signals: Establish a stable, discoverable local service topology so code/design substrate tools can connect to chat and embedding endpoints without reconfiguration.
novelty: Explicit reserved-port runway tied to candidate models, plus a security caveat about WSL/Windows localhost port forwarding.
contribution: A stable, human-readable port/service registry plus binding and endpoint conventions that downstream tooling can reference when wiring local AI services.
summary_for_embedding: ports.md is an operations file that maps local TCP ports to AI services, runtimes, and models, and documents OpenAI-compatible endpoints, 0.0.0.0 binding, and reserved future ports.
intention_for_embedding: The file was written to give the local substrate a canonical, discoverable service map for connecting clients to chat, embedding, and UI endpoints.
